content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Chain-of-Thought Prompting to Mitigate Social Bias in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often propagate social biases and stereotypes when prompted about demographic groups. Current prompting methods inadequately address these biases at generation time.",
  "motivation": "Biases in LLM outputs can perpetuate harmful stereotypes, leading to negative social impacts when these models are deployed. As LLMs increasingly interface with end-users, robust methods for on-the-fly bias mitigation are urgently needed. Prompting strategies are attractive for their ease of integration and minimal reliance on retraining or post-processing.",
  "method": "We propose 'Counterfactual Chain-of-Thought Prompting,' where the prompt explicitly asks the model to reason through its answer while also generating a counterfactual response for an alternative demographic group. The prompt instructs the model to compare both outputs and highlight differences attributable to group membership, then to revise its response to minimize unjustified disparities. For example, when asked about a profession and gender, the prompt requests the model to reason stepwise for both 'man' and 'woman,' analyze divergences, and generate a bias-mitigated answer. This method encourages the model to self-monitor and adjust for stereotypes during generation.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on bias measurement benchmarks such as StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs, as well as real-world QA datasets containing demographic references. Baselines will include zero-shot, standard CoT, and debiasing prompts. Metrics will include bias scores (e.g., stereotyping rate, log-likelihood gaps), answer utility, and human evaluations of stereotype presence."
}
